hypothesis: If crystal structures are encoded jointly as graph, RoboCrystallographer text, and simulated XRD modalities and trained with cross-modal latent prediction plus VIC regularization and contrastive loss, then the resulting model will achieve significantly lower MAE on Matbench crystal property tasks (band gap, shear modulus, bulk modulus, dielectric constant, and perovskite formation energy) and on AFLOW band gap prediction than single-modality or graph-based baselines (CGCNN, CoGN, ALIGNN), while requiring only a small fraction (~0.6%) of parameters to be updated during finetuning.
